Title: UV-B Responsive Flavonoid Synthesis Contributes to Tartary Buckwheat High-Altitude Adaption

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV-B protection; antioxidant protection against oxidative stress; contributes to high-altitude adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes quercetin formation and increases rutin-related metabolite synthesis, thereby enhancing UV-B tolerance and reducing ROS accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elevated expression in seeds; more stable expression than FtFLS3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression in Arabidopsis increased isoquercitrin and quercetin under UV-B assays
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: flavonol branch leading to quercetin-derived rutin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of flavonols quercetin and kaempferol from dihydroflavonol substrates taxifolin and aromadendrin.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for overexpression; native FtFLS4 promoter is UV-B inducible
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS candidate identification; enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis (G125D); hairy root overexpression; Arabidopsis overexpression; promoter-LUC assay; metabolite profiling; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Resequencing (>30-fold) of 200 F. tataricum core accessions; metabolite GWAS from Zhao et al. 2023; transcriptomics from public buckwheat datasets; WGCNA-integrated metabolomics
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: G125D variation strongly reduces catalytic activity and decreases quercetin/total flavonoids in hairy roots. FtFLS4 promoter is UV-B inducible, whereas homologous FgFLS4 shows high basal promoter activity but is not UV-B induced.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtFLS4 was identified as a major FLS homolog linked to quercetin-related metabolite variation in Tartary buckwheat. 2. FtFLS4 has stronger catalytic activity than FgFLS4 and converts taxifolin/aromadendrin to quercetin/kaempferol. 3. The G125D substitution markedly impairs activity and lowers flavonoid accumulation. 4. Its promoter is induced by UV-B. 5. Overexpression enhances rutin-related metabolites and UV-B tolerance while reducing ROS.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering UV-B-resilient crops by increasing quercetin precursor supply for rutin-pathway metabolites; favorable natural alleles avoiding G125D should be selected.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV-B protection; antioxidant protection against oxidative stress; contributes to high-altitude adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes isoquercitrin production and indirectly supports rutin accumulation, enhancing UV-B tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression positively correlates with seed rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression in Arabidopsis increased isoquercitrin and rutin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose to quercetin to form isoquercitrin, a key precursor of rutin.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for overexpression; native FtUF3GT1 promoter is UV-B inducible
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS; transcriptome-metabolome WGCNA; enzyme kinetics; promoter-LUC assay; molecular docking; site-directed mutagenesis (T225P as reported); hairy root overexpression; Arabidopsis overexpression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite GWAS from Zhao et al. 2023; integrated transcriptomics and metabolomics with WGCNA; public buckwheat transcriptome resources
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: FtUF3GT1 shows much stronger promoter activity and catalytic efficiency than homologues such as FgUF3GT19. Functional divergence likely reflects cumulative amino acid changes rather than a single residue. Paper text contains a possible mutation-position inconsistency (T225P/T255P in supplement mention).
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtUF3GT1 is significantly associated with flavonol glycoside variation and co-expressed in a rutin-correlated WGCNA module. 2. It catalyzes isoquercitrin synthesis from quercetin. 3. FtUF3GT1 has stronger promoter activity and higher catalytic efficiency than homologues. 4. Its promoter is UV-B responsive, likely due to abundant MYB recognition elements. 5. Overexpression raises isoquercitrin/rutin levels and improves UV-B tolerance with reduced ROS.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for boosting isoquercitrin and rutin accumulation and improving UV-B resilience; may be stacked with FtFLS4 and FtRT1 for pathway engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: UV-B protection; antioxidant protection against oxidative stress; contributes to high-altitude adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly promotes rutin formation; higher catalytic activity and UV-B-inducible promoter make it a major determinant of high rutin accumulation and UV-B tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression in Arabidopsis specifically increased rutin content
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: terminal rhamnosylation step of rutin synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes rutin formation from isoquercitrin and can also glycosylate multiple related flavonoid substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 UDP-glycosyltransferase with conserved PGSG box
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S (constitutive) for overexpression; native FtRT1 promoter is UV-B inducible
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mGWAS-linked candidate analysis; WGCNA; enzyme assay with multiple substrates; kinetic comparison with FgRT1; site-directed mutagenesis of PGSG-box-related residues; hairy root overexpression; Arabidopsis overexpression; promoter-LUC assay; pFtRT1:GUS in Arabidopsis; metabolite profiling; subcellular localization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolite GWAS from Zhao et al. 2023; integrated transcriptomics and metabolomics with WGCNA; public buckwheat transcriptome resources
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Amino acid residues in and near the PGSG box strongly affect activity. Mutations A54S, T86R and A357C reduce activity and rutin accumulation; V359A in FgRT1 increases activity. FtRT1 alone among tested RT homologues showed significant UV-B promoter induction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FtRT1 is associated with rutin-correlated genomic/coexpression signals and is seed-expressed. 2. It directly catalyzes rutin formation from isoquercitrin. 3. FtRT1 is substantially more active than FgRT1. 4. PGSG-box-associated residues are major determinants of catalytic efficiency and metabolite accumulation. 5. The FtRT1 promoter is UV-B inducible and its overexpression enhances rutin accumulation, lowers ROS, and improves UV-B tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value terminal-step engineering target for elevating rutin and UV-B tolerance; favorable PGSG-box alleles and UV-B-responsive promoter variants are promising for breeding.
